description_long: This object is a large solar carport structure featuring an array of blue photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The panels are arranged in a single, elongated row, elevated above the ground by a series of vertical and cross-braced supports. The frame appears to be constructed from steel or aluminum, providing both stability and durability for outdoor installation. The solar panels are tilted at a fixed angle to optimize sunlight capture, and the structure is open underneath, allowing for vehicle parking or shaded space. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images, suggesting these components may be concealed or located elsewhere. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for urban environments such as parking lots, public spaces, or commercial facilities.
description: A large, blue solar carport with a single row of tilted photovoltaic panels on a sturdy metal frame, elevated for parking or shade. The structure is open underneath and features cross-braced supports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the supporting frame, with clear visibility of the panel arrangement and open space below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the panels and the depth of the structure, with visible support posts and bracing.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the cross-braced metal supports and the underside of the panels, showing the structural framework.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the panel tilt and the linear arrangement, with supporting posts visible at the ends.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with steel or aluminum posts and cross-bracing
